articles.summary: Uruky - The Paid European Search Engine Uruky is a paid European search engine that uses a combination of plain old search and European search providers, such as Ecosia. It hosts its own servers in Europe, which may be seen as a way to improve digital sovereignty. While it solves some issues with traditional search engines like Kagi, such as the use of Russian Yandex service, it also has limitations, including not being able to offer AI-powered results and having limited customization options.

articles.title: Matrix Orthogonalization Improves Memory in Recurrent Models
articles.summary: Transformers exhibit remarkable associative recall abilities due to attention providing each token direct access to those preceding it, a mechanism that has been hard for other architectures like recurrent neural networks to match. However, in long-horizon reinforcement learning applications such as Dreamer, making recurrent neural networks work while maintaining associative recall is essential. The best known RNN for associative recall is mLSTM, which demonstrates substantially improved recall over baselines on one benchmark MQAR, but pure recall may not be sufficient to measure recurrent performance. By orthogonalizing the matrix memory of mLSTMs during reads and training with this additional process, researchers have found that it improves NAR performance on next-token prediction using MAD noisy AR samples.

articles.title: The first early human eggs from stem cells
articles.summary: The first early human eggs from stem cells have been generated through a process of converting blood cells into stem cells, which are then coaxed into becoming miniature human ovaries containing the early eggs. This breakthrough has the potential to redefine fertility by making viable eggs from stem cells, a technology that can already be achieved in mice and has far-reaching implications for human application. The development of this technology could create new options for families seeking to have healthy children without relying on hormone injections or surgical retrieval, enabling women to have children at an older age.

articles.title: Forestiere Underground Gardens
articles.summary: The Forestiere Underground Gardens in Fresno, California are a series of subterranean structures built by Baldassare Forestiere over a period of 40 years from 1906 to his death in 1946, operated by members of the Forestiere family through the Forestiere Historical Center. This unconventional example of vernacular architecture has been recognized as a U.S. National Historic Place and California Historical Landmark since its listing on the National Register of Historic Places in 1977.

articles.title: The Threat of Residential Proxies
articles.summary: The rise of residential proxies has become a significant concern for developers as they require reliable and efficient methods to scrape websites without being detected. Residential proxies, also known as legitimate proxy services, can be used for paid network monitoring but have been exploited by criminals seeking to hide their tracks. The increasing demand for AI-powered scraping tools has further fueled this issue, with some vendors attempting to train on website content using bots that generate more internet traffic than humans. This raises questions about the economy of scraping and whether a payment protocol can be implemented to balance the need for legitimate proxy services with the demands of malicious actors.

articles.title: Generating the P3 Tiling
articles.summary: Penrose tilings use Robinson triangles with subdivision rules that guarantee pairs of acute and obtuse triangles in each round of subdivision, resulting in rhombii patterns. articles.title: What's wrong with EU age verification? (Nothing)
articles.summary: Online age verification is not just a technical issue, but also an ideological one. The European Union's approach to implementing age restrictions online raises concerns about the potential for mass surveillance and manipulation of vulnerable individuals. A 9- or 10-year-old child wandering the internet without limits is not just a matter of parental choice, as teenagers also require spaces to act independently and make decisions. Implementing strict age verification measures can be effective in limiting access to sensitive content, but it raises concerns about the potential for online identity checkpoints and mass surveillance.
